2.title: Part II | An Age of Great Heroes

3.title: Part III | The Mysterious Gentleman Thief

4.title: Part IV | Now, The King Must Die

chapters.name: The Man with the Bug in His Arm
chapters.tip: Istuis - the high-tech hub of Eydtea - supplies the kingdoms with transportation and communication technologies, as well as military equipment.

chapters.name: The Silver Star
chapters.tip: Astrum is Anoleth's capital city, home to the Chrysalis - the oldest castle structure in the world, and seat of the King.

chapters.name: New Dawn
chapters.tip: The rebel group now known as New Dawn have warred with the Anolethic monarchy for close to a century and a half, inspired against the cruelty of the early kings of Anoleth.

chapters.name: Voices
chapters.tip: Centridyne engines are an innovation of Tecma-Centro Industries in Istuis, alleged to allow vehicles to hover at low energy costs by distributing weight through centrifugal forces.

chapters.name: His Father
chapters.tip: The previous King of Anoleth, Lor'End Lucien, was a brutal but effective monarch, losing his control only in the weeks leading up to his murder by New Dawn rebels.

chapters.name: The Lion and the Swift
chapters.tip: The Lords' Judiciary is the legal system governing Anoleth - except its capital, where the Judge Superior rules on crime and punishment.

chapters.name: Fort Balliway
chapters.tip: Menyantis and Anoleth are separated by the Yarimot River, which ends at the Freyne Channel toward the North Wilder Sea.

chapters.name: Obria
chapters.tip: The Crown Judiciary command a number of coalition strongholds with the Lords, in order to transfer prisoners between the two systems.

chapters.name: There is a Tale to be Told, Here
chapters.tip: Lord Jhannai is the last of his species, the Kasseian, a daemonic race of winged giants created during the warring age of the Old Kings, and destroyed by Anoleth.
